record.field: AzuraCast is a self-hosted, all-in-one web radio management suite that turns a small server into a fully operational internet radio station in minutes.

AzuraCast is a self-hosted, all-in-one web radio management suite that turns a small server into a fully operational internet radio station in minutes.

It bundles the complete broadcasting stack — streaming servers, an automated DJ, a media library and a modern web control panel — behind a single Docker-based installer. Built for stations of any size, it runs comfortably on affordable VPS hosts and lets non-technical operators manage scheduling, playlists and live shows without touching the command line. It is developed entirely by humans and released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

Key features
  • Turnkey broadcasting stack with Icecast, SHOUTcast and RSAS frontends plus a Liquidsoap-powered AutoDJ
  • Web-based media library with playlist scheduling, song requests and crossfade/ReplayGain processing
  • Live DJ streaming over a browser-based Web DJ or external clients, with seamless handoff to AutoDJ
  • Real-time listener analytics, historical reports and Now Playing metadata feeds
  • Manage multiple independent stations from one panel, each with its own users and permissions
  • Full REST API, public station pages, embeddable players and podcast hosting
Best for

Independent broadcasters, community and online radio stations, podcasters and hosting providers that want full ownership of their streaming infrastructure without relying on third-party radio platforms.

services: azuracast: image: ghcr.io/azuracast/azuracast:latest restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' volumes: - ./azuracast_station_data:/var/azuracast/stations - ./azuracast_data:/var/azuracast/www_tmp - ./azuracast_uploads:/var/azuracast/uploads - ./azuracast_backups:/var/azuracast/backups environment: - LANG=en_US.UTF-8 - AZURACAST_DC_REVISION=1 - MYSQL_HOST=mariadb - MYSQL_PORT=3306 - MYSQL_USER=azuracast - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ mysql_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE=azuracast depends_on: - mariadb container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mariadb: image: mariadb:11.4 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./mariadb_data:/var/lib/mysql environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ mysql_root_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE=azuracast - MYSQL_USER=azuracast - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ mysql_password }} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mariadb' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mariadb # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
